disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing lower back and bilateral hip pain of over three years, disproportionate to a minor original injury, with an acute worsening this week after minor exertion
trigger_question_examples: How long have you had this pain?
What was happening when it got worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been there for over three years since a small injury, but it flared up badly yesterday after I did a bit too much, and today I just can't move.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute total loss of mobility — unable to rise from the chair or safely reach the bathroom or kitchen — in a person who lives alone
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to stand or walk at all right now?
Is there anyone at home with you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't get up out of the chair without terrible pain, I can't get to the toilet or the kitchen, and I live on my own so there's no one here to help.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Normal bowel and bladder control, no numbness around the saddle/groin area, no sudden leg weakness
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any loss of control of your bladder or bowels?
Any numbness around your saddle area, or sudden weakness in your legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — my waterworks and bowels are normal and I don't have any numbness down below or sudden weakness, it's just the pain stopping me moving.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, chills, rigors or feeling systemically unwell
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, chills or shakes?
Do you feel unwell in yourself, or just the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shakes, I don't feel sick or hot — it's really just the pain and being stuck.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sudden severe traumatic event and no acute loss of height associated with the flare
trigger_question_examples: Did you have any fall or sudden injury?
Was there a sudden snap or crack in your back?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fall or sudden accident — I just overdid it a little yesterday, there was no big injury or snap.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe insomnia and non-restorative sleep for several days with profound fatigue
trigger_question_examples: Are you feeling tired or run down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't slept properly in days, and I'm completely exhausted and drained.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prominent low mood, tearfulness, anxiety and irritability related to the pain and disability
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping in yourself with all this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm at the end of my tether — I keep crying, I feel so anxious and irritable, I just can't take much more of this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thoughts of self-harm or of not wanting to go on when sensitively asked
trigger_question_examples: When things feel this overwhelming, do you ever have thoughts of not wanting to go on, or of harming yourself?
Have you had any thoughts that life isn't worth living, or of ending things?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'd never do anything like that — I just want the pain to stop so I can get back to normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Escalating reliance on simple analgesics (paracetamol and NSAIDs at maximum daily dose) with diminishing effect
trigger_question_examples: What have you tried for the pain so far?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm taking the most I'm allowed of my paracetamol and anti-inflammatories every day, but they barely do anything now.
